diff --git a/Cargo.toml b/Cargo.toml
index a5679ded..edc64d77 100644
--- a/Cargo.toml
+++ b/Cargo.toml
@@ -41,9 +41,9 @@ serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
 # whitespace, key order, or escape preferences the producer chose.
 # Enabled here in Phase A so PR-B2 can land as a pure consumer change.
 serde_json = { version = "1", features = ["preserve_order", "arbitrary_precision", "raw_value"] }
-thiserror = "1"
+thiserror = "2"
 tracing = "0.1"
-pyo3 = { version = "0.24", features = ["abi3-py310"] }
+pyo3 = { version = "0.29", features = ["abi3-py310"] }
 # NOTE: the proxy-era workspace deps (clap, tokio, axum, tower, reqwest,
 # anyhow, bytes, aws-sigv4/-config/-credential-types/-smithy-runtime-api,
 # gcp_auth) were removed with the proxy/parity crates in the amputation
diff --git a/crates/furl-core/Cargo.toml b/crates/furl-core/Cargo.toml
index 6221e94d..dc339ac9 100644
--- a/crates/furl-core/Cargo.toml
+++ b/crates/furl-core/Cargo.toml
@@ -11,14 +11,14 @@ serde = { workspace = true }
 serde_json = { workspace = true }
 thiserror = { workspace = true }
 tracing = { workspace = true }
-tiktoken-rs = "0.11"
+tiktoken-rs = "0.12"
 # `md5` for the CCR cache_key. Python's compression_store hashes the original
 # diff with MD5 truncated to 24 hex chars; we must match byte-for-byte.
 md-5 = "0.10"
 # `sha2` for `hash_field_name` (SHA256 truncated to 8 hex chars) and the
 # crusher's row hashes (sha256[:6]). Python uses `hashlib.sha256` so we
 # need byte-exact parity.
-sha2 = "0.10"
+sha2 = "0.11"
 # `dashmap` for the CCR storage backend. Concurrent HashMap with sharded
 # locking — distinct keys hashed to different shards never contend, so
 # concurrent multi-threaded load doesn't queue on a single Mutex. Lock-free
